title: Project-lifecycle vocabulary — closing the three-way status drift with a distinct PROJECT_LIFECYCLE enum (prior art: how spec processes define maturity label sets)
summary: Prep research for decision #2134 (surfaced by the #2088 grounding digest; reserved by the #portfolio-project-tiering lineage as 'a separate statute-amendment decision'). The question: the project status axis is named by three different vocabularies — the data convention (concept 17 / poc 24 / draft 4 across the 45 we:src/_data/projects/*.json entries), the public five-stage ladder concept->poc->draft->candidate->stable (we:src/project-lifecycle.njk:11-69, with consumer-facing semantics like 'API is frozen'), and the descriptor enum LIFECYCLE={concept,draft,experimental,active} (we:scripts/check-standards-rules.mjs:789) whose STATUS_SYNONYMS deprecates 'stable'->'active' — and the statute DELIBERATELY keeps project status outside LIFECYCLE (we:docs/agent/platform-decisions.md:136-143 rule 6: 'poc is the convention — project status is not enum-validated'), so closing the drift is a statute amendment. CODE GROUND TRUTH: the data is clean (45/45 values already inside the public ladder — the drift is definitional, not in entries, so a ladder-matching enum is zero-migration); the literal 'concept' is load-bearing (D3-readiness demotion at we:src/_data/backlog.js:114-120 keys relatedProjectStatus==='concept'); render is axis-agnostic (the projectStatus macro we:src/_includes/project-status.njk serves BOTH projects and plug/state descriptor pages, and we:src/css/style.css:962-969 provisions meter fills for the union incl. candidate/stable); a stray FOURTH spelling 'speculative' sits in the orphaned pre-#1157 root spec we:src/_data/webhandlers.json:5 (dead file, no consuming template) — what an unvalidated convention produces; the sibling axis precedent is tier, enum-validated day one (PROJECT_TIERS + validateProjectTier, #2088 Fork 3, 'unlike status'); descriptor 'active' is implementation-flavored (status:active blocks must carry implementedBy, we:scripts/check-standards.mjs:172) — a meaning a zero-impl WE project (#1282) cannot take. PRIOR ART: every surveyed process defines its maturity label set ONCE, normatively, as a closed set — W3C maturity levels (WD->CR->PR->REC, Process Document), TC39 stages 0-4 (process doc + validated proposals table), Node.js stability index 0-3 (machine-readable docs metadata), USWDS/Polaris/Carbon component lifecycles (closed enums enforced by docs tooling) — and distinct TRACKS keep distinct LADDERS (W3C does not reuse TC39's stages; Node's index is not npm's). The counter-model is npm dist-tags: publisher-declared free-form labels, no closed set — exactly where ecosystem drift lives ('latest' must be special-cased). Pattern: declared value + one closed normative definition + mechanical validation; drift arises where the label set lives in prose, data, and tooling separately — WE's current shape. SYNTHESIS -> forks: Fork 1 (canonical vocabulary) a DISTINCT PROJECT_LIFECYCLE={concept,poc,draft,candidate,stable} matching the public ladder — zero migration, D3 literal untouched, per-track ladders per prior art, spec-stability semantics vs the descriptor's implementation semantics — vs merging projects into descriptor LIFECYCLE (rejected: 24-file poc->experimental migration, public-page rewrite, imports the stable->active synonym so the catalog could never say the word its own ladder promises as terminal) vs coining a fresh third set (rejected broken: a fourth vocabulary). Fork 2 (enforcement) enum-validate project status in check:standards, error severity, day one, no synonym map (data already clean; tier precedent adjacent in the same file; status rules 'never warn' by house design) — vs convention-only (rejected: the exact gap that produced the drift) vs warn-only (rejected: contradicts the never-warn shape). NOT forks: statute amendment mechanics (codification at ratification, #1886); check placement/naming (impl detail, #2030); auto-deriving the ladder page from the enum (declared-over-auto-derived, #2074 — the gate guaranteeing data⊆enum is the drift that matters); cleanup of the orphaned root specs webhandlers.json/webportals.json (follow-up build). Word-reuse caution: 'stable' becomes canonical-for-Project while staying a deprecated descriptor synonym — acceptable because checks are kind-keyed, but the amendment must state the reuse explicitly.
